page: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Edsv7 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz and is equipped with a large 480 MB L3 cache. The system is provisioned with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. It includes 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-threaded capabilities, with gzip compression reaching 35.21 GB/sec, placing it in the top tier of tested instances. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is also strong at 14.51 GB/sec. Multi-threaded operations, such as stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and bzip3 decompression, fall within the average range. Memory bandwidth tests show 670.08 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 85.42 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Database operations and Redis SET operations both perform at average levels.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for tile and int8 operations, which assist in localized vector and matrix math. This is reflected in average LLM inference speeds of 63.85 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 7B model. Given its high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU and bundled 472 GB local storage, the E8ds_v7 offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. It is well-suited for relational databases, in-memory caching layers like Redis, data compression tasks, and moderate web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine configured with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-optimized 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz with a 480 MB L3 cache, it features 472 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage. The server lacks a GPU but includes advanced CPU instruction sets like Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmarks show top-tier single-threaded gzip compression at 35.21 GB/sec and strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average tier. With its high memory density and local storage, the E8ds_v7 is a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for memory-intensive workloads, including relational databases, in-memory caching, data compression, and moderate web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB of RAM, 472 GB of local storage, and advanced Intel AMX instructions for memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E8ds_v7 is an 8-vCPU memory-optimized VM with 64 GB RAM, 472 GB local storage, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring high-frequency Intel Xeon processing, dense RAM, and fast local storage for database workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor
64.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
472 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression performance at 35.21 GB/sec
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for optimized vector math
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and data compression
